notes:
I'm starting the game, the game launches, Unity Engine logo shows up, then it fades away and proceeds to a completely black screen, has no sound, and the only text I see is "development build" in bottom right corner. When I open the Unity console inside of the game it constantly sends the same error message: "NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object". Also I tried to launch the game through Lutris both with Wine and Proton and in the terminal I had these errors:
err:   wsi: parseColorimetryInfo: Failed to get parse edid.
err:   DXGI: Failed to parse display metadata + colorimetry info, using blank.
err:   wsi: parseColorimetryInfo: Failed to get parse edid.
err:   DXGI: Failed to parse display metadata + colorimetry info, using blank.

The game itself didn't release yet, right now it is in closed pre-alpha testing stage, which opened up yesterday, and currently the pre-alpha build goes under a different name in Steam, is hidden and only distributed with Steam product keys. You can obtain them by signing up to the game closed pre-alpha test.
title: Starlight Revolver

notes: Got invited to the alpha testing via Steam. Game launches, shows Unity logo, then goes to a black screen with "Development build" on the lower right corner.
title: Starlight Revolver

notes: It opens and that's as far as you'll get. They said on their discord they do not support linux right now so there is 0 chance of getting past this black screen thing. Really sad.
title: Starlight Revolver

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6650 XT (radeonsi, navi23, LLVM 19.1.7, DRM 3.61, 6.14.10-arch1-1)
notes: The game opens on a black screen. That's it. It appears as the devs don't care about linux support, currently at least. I'll be looking forward to see if they'll eventually support linux, if they don't, then i'll simply never play it.
